title: HIV-1 NC-POL Amplification and Oxford Nanopore Sequencing for Drug Resistance Analysis
author: Nicola Coetzee

steps.instruction: Prepare the RNA-primer Mix and RT Mix as following: Reagent Final concentration Volume per reaction Volume per reaction 1 dNTPs 10 mM 0.5 mM 1 2 Reverse primer set R1a:R1b 10 µM (1:1) 0.25 µM 1 3 Nuclease-free water - - 1 4 Template RNA 10 5 Total volume 13 RNA-primer Mix Reagent Final concentration Volume per reaction Volume per reaction 1 5x SSIV buffer 5X 1X 4 2 DTT 0.1 M 0.01 M 1 3 Ribonuclease inhibitor 40 U/μL 40 U 1 4 Superscript IV Reverse Transcriptase 200 U/ μL 50 U 1 5 Total volume 7 RT Mix

steps.instruction: Prepare Pre-nested PCR Master Mix as follow: Reagent Final concentration Volume per reaction Volume per reaction 1 SuperFi II PCR Master Mix 2X 1X 10 2 Forward primer set: F1a:F1b 20 µM (1:1) 0.4 µM 0.4 3 Reverse primer set: R1a:R1b 20 µM (1:1) 0.4 µM 0.4 4 Nuclease-free water 4.2 5 cDNA 5 6 Total volume 20 Pre-nested Master Mix

substeps.instruction: Prepare Pre-nested PCR Master Mix as follow: Reagent Final concentration Volume per reaction Volume per reaction 1 SuperFi II PCR Master Mix 2X 1X 10 2 Forward primer set: F1a:F1b 20 µM (1:1)
5 cDNA 5 6 Total volume 20 Pre-nested Master Mix

steps.instruction: Prepare the Nested Master Mix as follow: %s %s
This section describes bead-based purification of PCR products using AMPure XP magnetic beads .
Prepare sequencing libraries using the ONT Native Barcoding Kit 96 V14 (SQK-NBD114.96) protocol. Sequencing library size (≤30 amplicons per Flongle recommended).

This section describes analysis of Oxford Nanopore sequencing data using bioinformatic pipelines and sequence analysis software for consensus sequence generation and HIV drug resistance interpretation.
Add 3 µL of antisense mix into each PCR tube.
Add 10 µL of RNA to RNA-primer mix.
Incubate at 65 °C for 00:05:00 and immediately cool on ice for at least 00:02:00 .
Add 7 µL of RT mix to RNA-primer mix.
Incubate at 50 °C for 00:50:00 and 85 °C for 00:05:00 .
Add 20 µL of cDNA to 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube.
This section describes purification of cDNA using RNAClean XP magnetic beads.
Add 36 µL of RNAClean XP beads to reaction volume (1.8× cDNA volume).
Incubate at Room temperature for 00:10:00 .
Place tubes on a magnetic rack and wait for liquid to clear ~ 00:05:00 .
Remove and discard supernatant.
Wash beads 3× with 250 µL of 70% EtOH .
Air dry for ~ 00:02:00 .
Briefly centrifuge and remove any remaining EtOH using a P10 pipette without disturbing the bead pellet.
Resuspend beads in 20 µL 5 millimolar (mM) Tris-HCl.
Briefly centrifuge and incubate on shaker at Room temperature for 00:05:00 .
Place tubes on a magnetic rack and wait for liquid to clear ~ 00:02:00 .
Transfer elute to a new microcentrifuge tube.
Aliquot 15 µL of Pre-nested Master Mix into each PCR tube.
Add 5 µL of purified cDNA to master mix. Template Adjustment: HIV-1 plasma viral load <50 000 copies/mL: use undiluted cDNA HIV-1 plasma viral load ≥50 000 copies/mL: dilute cDNA 1:20

Seal and spin tubes/plate down and run PCR following the conditions below Step Temperature (°C) Duration Cycles 1 Phase I 2 Initial denature 98 2 minutes 1 3 Phase II 4 Denature 98 10 secondes 5 5 Anneal 58 15 seconds 6 Extension 72 3 minutes 7 Phase III (touchdown) 8 Denature 98 10 seconds 15 9 Anneal (Decrease by 0.5°C each cycle 58 15 seconds 10 Extension 72 3 minutes 11 Phase IV 12 Denature 98 10 seconds 15 13 Anneal 50 15 seconds 14 Extension 72 3 minutes 15 Phase V 16 Final extension 72 6 minutes Thermocycling condition for NC-POL PCR
Aliquot 15 µL of Nested Master Mix into each PCR reaction.
Dilute Pre-nested PCR product 1:50 using 5 millimolar (mM) Tris-HCl .
Add 5 µL of diluted Pre-nested product to Nested PCR reaction.
Run same thermocycling conditions as for the Pre-nested PCR (Step 4.3). Expected Product: Confirm amplified product size of ~3.5 kb by agarose gel electrophoresis.
Transfer 16 µL of Nested PCR product to DNA LoBind tube.
Incubate on shaking incubator at 900 rpm, Room temperature, for 00:05:00 .
Place on magnetic rack and wait for liquid to clear ~ 00:02:00 .
Remove and discard supernatant.
Wash beads 2× with 250 µL of 80% EtOH .
Briefly centrifuge and remove remaining EtOH using a P10 pipette without disturbing the bead pellet.
Air dry for ~ 00:03:00 .
Resuspend beads in 20 µL 5 millimolar (mM) Tris-HCl .
Briefly centrifuge and incubate on shaking incubator at 900 rpm, 37 °C, for 00:05:00 .
Analysis pipeline: Nano-RECall for the POL region and Nano_RECall_NC for the Gag-NC region. Adequate sequencing coverage across POL and NC region (400 X).
Translated NC consensus sequence mutation analysis: Geneious Prime.
Add 28.8 µL of AMPure XP beads to reaction volume (1.8× Nested PCR volume after gel electrophoresis).
Place tubes on a magnetic rack and wait for liquid to clear ~ 00:02:00 .

Transfer elute to a new DNA LoBind tube. Quantification Measure DNA using Qubit dsDNA HS Assay on a Qubit fluorometer. Recommended input for sequencing: 200 fmol (455 ng for 3.5 kb amplicon). DNA concentrations should be used for equimolar pooling prior to sequencing.
Drug resistance interpretation: Stanford University HIV Drug Resistance Database.

Load onto R10.4.1 Flongle (FLO-FLG114) or R10.4.1 flow cell (FLO-MIN114) and run on GridION with the following run parameters: Standard run time: 24 hours High accuracy basecalling Trim barcodes Size selection: Read length (2.5 kb – 4.5 kb) Qscore: 9

substeps.instruction: flow cell (FLO-MIN114) and run on GridION with the following run parameters: Standard run time: 24 hours High accuracy basecalling Trim barcodes Size selection: Read length (

steps.instruction: Pipette mix carefully until pellet resuspended.
Incubate without mixing for > 00:05:00 .

Resuspend RNA pellet in ice cold 5 millimolar (mM) Tris-HCl according to the pre-determined plasma viral load: HIV-RNA plasma viral load (copies/mL) Volume of 5mM Tris-HCl (µL) 1 20 2 50 000 - 100 000 35 3 >100 000 50 *If viral load is unknown, use 20 µL resuspension volume.
Air dry for 00:15:00 .
Briefly centrifuge and remove any remaining EtOH using a P10 pipette.
Wash the pellet 3× with 1000 µL of 70% EtOH . Centrifuge at 21100 x g, 10 °C, for 00:20:00 during the first two washes and 00:10:00 during the final wash.
Centrifuge at 21100 x g, 10 °C, for 00:20:00 .
Add 600 µL of 100% Isopropanol and mix by inversion (30 times).
Incubate at Room temperature for 00:10:00 .
Vortex briefly and spin down tubes. DO NOT VIGOROUSLY VORTEX.
Add 600 µL of Viral Lysis Buffer .
Remove and discard supernatant using a fine-tip pasteur pipette.
Centrifuge at 24000 x g, 10 °C, for 01:00:00 .
Transfer supernatant to a new microcentrifuge tube.
Centrifuge at 2700 x g, 4 °C, for 00:15:00 .
Add 0.5-1 mL plasma to a 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube.
This section describes ethanol precipitation and purification of viral RNA from plasma.
